commit.message:
fix(resurrect): keep autosave helper alive through a save cycle

Replace the one-shot autosave status renderer with a single autosave command
that acquires the autosave lock for its full lifetime, waits until the next
due save or icon-clear event, and writes status output over time before
exiting. Keep autosave-status as a compatibility alias, but route both names
through the same implementation and update the command-level tests to match.

This avoids spawning a brand new autosave process on every status refresh
while still letting tmux own the helper lifecycle. The lifetime lock now
prevents duplicate helpers from doing work, and each fresh invocation still
picks up the latest interval and icon settings on the next cycle.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(resurrect): add status-line autosaves and typed restore rows

Add automatic session snapshots that are driven by a tmux status-right helper instead of a long-running daemon.

This wires a new autosave-status subcommand through the main entrypoint, resolves autosave interval, retention, icon, and pane-content settings from tmux options or environment variables, performs due-save checks under a nonblocking lock, updates the default restore target, and prunes only older autosaves. main.tmux also now publishes the resolved binary path so status-right snippets can invoke the plugin without guessing its install location.

The restore-from menu now records save kind in metadata, shows typed rows with the snapshot name first, uses distinct colours for manual and automatic saves, and renders styled rows through the ANSI-aware width path so coloured entries do not pick up bogus trailing ellipses.

commit.message:
[gpu] Fix draw encode blob ownership leaks

hb_gpu_draw_encode() could allocate a fresh output buffer and then
return NULL before transferring ownership to a blob, leaking that
temporary buffer on late encode failures.

The recycled-blob growth path also tried hb_realloc() first and fell
back to hb_malloc() when that failed. Once the new buffer was committed
to the recycled blob, the old recycled buffer could be orphaned.

Free temporary buffers on the remaining late-failure exits and keep
the old recycled buffer alive until a replacement has been committed.
Tighten the GPU API recycle test to encode a larger second outline so
that reused-buffer growth stays covered.

Tested:
- meson test -C build

Assisted-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(preview): render pane cursors from visible pane captures

Add cursor-aware pane previews for pane, window, session, and tree preview paths. The tmux layer now captures visible pane rows, carries cursor coordinates with the preview data, and the UI overlays a white block cursor when the cursor falls inside the rendered preview.

The implementation history on this branch matters to the final shape:
- an initial history-relative capture approach rendered the cursor in the wrong place because it mixed capture output with visible-screen cursor coordinates
- a follow-up attempt using capture-pane with -E -1 truncated previews badly and hid content in sparse panes
- another revision still scrolled sparse single-line panes onto blank trailing rows, making the only content line disappear

The final design fixes those failures by treating the preview as visible pane rows only, slicing around the last meaningful visible row or cursor row, and anchoring pane preview scroll state to the last non-empty content row so sparse panes stay visible.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

- several runtime paths still had production-grade correctness and operability issues around idle waiting, signal wakeup, and sampler publication ordering

commit.message:
refactor(color): replace csscolorparser with local subset (#13)

## Why
- Issue #12 calls out CSS color parsing as compatibility-critical
behavior that should live in this repository.
- Keeping the parser local makes coverage and upstream-backed tests
useful for finding missing color cases.

## What
- Replace the `csscolorparser` dependency with a crate-local CSS color
subset parser used by typed color parsing and brightness calculation.
- Keep the supported surface focused on the formats this crate currently
relies on for upstream-compatible behavior.
- Add Rust-side contract tests for accepted and rejected color inputs
alongside the existing upstream-backed reference suite.

## Testing
- `just test`

Fixes #12

commit.message:
feat(cli): 支持长输出接入外部 pager (#7)

## Why
- `kat` 之前只会一次性把完整 ANSI 输出写到 stdout，长文件场景下缺少可用的翻页体验。
- 这个改动先把外部 pager 路径做扎实，保持现有 renderer 继续只负责产出 ANSI 文本。

## What
- 新增 `--paging=auto|always|never`，默认 `auto`，并且只在普通渲染模式下评估是否进入 pager。
- 重构 CLI 输出链路：先汇总完整输出，再统一决定直写 stdout 还是交给外部 pager。
- 优先读取 `PAGER`，未设置时默认回退到 `less -R -F -X`，并为自动分页路径保留直接输出回退。
- 补充分页参数解析、自动分页判定和 pager stdin 写入相关测试，并同步更新 README 与架构文档。

## Testing
- `just test`

## Notes
- 关联 issue: #3

commit.message:
fix(shadow_mode): use decision.action instead of decision.allowed (#109)

* fix(shadow_mode): use decision.action instead of decision.allowed

decision.allowed is never set by _build_decision(); it always sets
decision.action. Reading decision.allowed always yields nil, causing
would_reject=true and original_action='reject' for every request —
including ones that would have been allowed.

Fix: derive was_allowed from decision.action ~= 'reject', which correctly
handles all three states (allow, throttle, reject).

Fixes #108

* fix(shadow_mode): preserve allowed-based compatibility

commit.message:
fix(token_bucket): pass TTL to dict:set to prevent unbounded key accumulation (fixes #106) (#107)

dict:set was called without expiry, so bucket state keys persisted in shared
memory indefinitely. As users come and go, stale entries accumulate until the
dict fills and LRU eviction becomes unpredictable.

TTL = ceil(burst / tokens_per_second) * 2 — the time to fully refill an empty
bucket, doubled. Same pattern as cost_budget.lua line 274.
